snippet: Transfer your work to Google AI Studio or Google Antigravity. MY CONSOLE Hi Denys, You're receiving this communication because you are a user of Firebase Studio, which launched as a preview product
subject: [Action Advised] Migrate Firebase Studio projects by Mar 22, 2027
body:
Hi Denys,

You’re receiving this communication because you are a user of Firebase  
Studio, which launched as a preview product in 2025.

To streamline our AI-driven development tools, we’ll be shutting down  
Firebase Studio on March 22, 2027. Since its core capabilities are already  
built into Google AI Studio and Google Antigravity, we recommend you  
transfer your Firebase Studio projects to these products.

We have provided additional information below to help you navigate this  
transition and ensure your projects are migrated before the deadline.

What you need to know

Key changes and timelines:

    - March 19, 2026: Firebase Studio will enter its shutdown phase. While  
the product remains fully functional, additional tools will become  
available to help you transfer your workspaces into Google AI Studio or  
Google Antigravity.
    - June 22, 2026: Final date to create new workspaces or register new  
accounts. These features will be disabled after this date to prepare for  
the final shutdown.
    - March 22, 2027: Firebase Studio will be shut down and will no longer  
be accessible via the Firebase Studio product URL. This is the final  
deadline to transfer or archive your data before all remaining code becomes  
inaccessible.

Potential impact:

    - Migration: You can transfer projects to Google AI studio or Google  
Antigravity until March 22, 2027. Any code not migrated by this date will  
be inaccessible.
    - Data Limitations: Agent chat history (trajectories) cannot be migrated  
to the new platforms.
    - Service Continuity: Core Firebase services (Firestore, Auth, App  
Hosting) are not affected and will continue to operate normally.

What you need to do

Action advised:

    - Start new workspaces or projects elsewhere: We strongly recommend that  
you start new workspaces or projects directly in AI Studio (if you want to  
use a web IDE) or Google Antigravity (for desktop app users).
    - Workspaces started through the App Prototyping agent: We recommend  
moving apps created or vibe coded with the App Prototyping agent to Google  
AI Studio. To facilitate this, we will be rolling out a new "Transfer to AI  
Studio" button within your workspace. While we don’t have exact timing for  
this rollout, the feature will appear in your workspace once the update is  
live.
    - Workspaces started through the App Prototyping agent, Firebase Studio  
template or a blank workspace: For projects requiring an IDE experience, we  
recommend transitioning to Google Antigravity. You can use the Zip &  
Download feature to save your workspace to your computer. Once saved, you  
can open your code in Google Antigravity.


Refer to the Firebase Studio Migration Guide[1] to move your projects to  
Google AI Studio or Antigravity.

We’re here to help

If you have any questions or require assistance, please contact Firebase  
support[2] or the Community Forum[3].

Thanks,

The Firebase Studio team

[1] https://firebase.google.com/docs/studio/migrating-project
[2] https://firebase.google.com/support/troubleshooter/studio/help
[3] https://community.firebasestudio.dev/

© 2026 Google LLC 1600 Amphitheatre Parkway, Mountain View, CA 94043

You have received this mandatory service announcement to update you about  
important changes to Firebase or your account.
